raw.title: Undefined borrowed concept "blocked policy" embedded in a stop condition
raw.evidence_summary: `Stop: the goal is met, the turn/attempt limit is reached, or the platform-specific blocked policy says to stop.` The opening caveat only neutralizes *command names* from the source article ("Treat platform-specific command names from that article as concepts"), not borrowed *policies*.
raw.impact: An agent filling/executing a goal loop that has never read the source article will either ignore this third stop condition or hallucinate a meaning. Stop conditions are safety-critical; undefined terms there are exactly the "ambiguous steps" the lens flags.
raw.suggested_action: Replace with plain language, e.g. "or a hard platform block (rate limit, safety block, quota) prevents progress," or drop it since the other two conditions already cover the realistic cases.
raw.raw_text:
- File: `SKILL.md` (section "2. Goal-Based Loop", `Stop:` line)
- Evidence: `Stop: the goal is met, the turn/attempt limit is reached, or the platform-specific blocked policy says to stop.` The opening caveat only neutralizes *command names* from the source article ("Treat platform-specific command names from that article as concepts"), not borrowed *policies*.
- Impact: An agent filling/executing a goal loop that has never read the source article will either ignore this third stop condition or hallucinate a meaning. Stop conditions are safety-critical; undefined terms there are exactly the "ambiguous steps" the lens flags.
- Suggested fix: Replace with plain language, e.g. "or a hard platform block (rate limit, safety block, quota) prevents progress," or drop it since the other two conditions already cover the realistic cases.

raw.title: Source attribution is present but fragile and unverifiable
raw.evidence_summary: The entire conceptual model is sourced to a single walled-garden link: `Source read for this skill: WeChat translation "...", 宝玉AI, 2026-07-07, https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/...`. No canonical English original is cited; the URL is the kind that rots, geo-restricts, or edits silently, and I could not fetch it here.
raw.impact: A future reader/agent cannot verify that the turn/goal/time/proactive taxonomy and the role-separation diagram actually came from "Getting started with loops." If the link decays, the skill's foundational claim becomes uncheckable. (The doc is hedged, so this is verifiability, not an over-claim.)
raw.suggested_action: If a canonical English source exists, cite it alongside the WeChat translation; label the date as "accessed 2026-07-07" to disambiguate from publish date; and inline-summarize the model so the skill does not depend on the link resolving.
raw.raw_text:
- File: `SKILL.md` (intro, after "Getting started with loops")
- Evidence: The entire conceptual model is sourced to a single walled-garden link: `Source read for this skill: WeChat translation "...", 宝玉AI, 2026-07-07, https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/...`. No canonical English original is cited; the URL is the kind that rots, geo-restricts, or edits silently, and I could not fetch it here.
- Impact: A future reader/agent cannot verify that the turn/goal/time/proactive taxonomy and the role-separation diagram actually came from "Getting started with loops." If the link decays, the skill's foundational claim becomes uncheckable. (The doc is hedged, so this is verifiability, not an over-claim.)
- Suggested fix: If a canonical English source exists, cite it alongside the WeChat translation; label the date as "accessed 2026-07-07" to disambiguate from publish date; and inline-summarize the model so the skill does not depend on the link resolving.

raw.title: No runnable in-session example despite in-session execution being the primary capability
raw.evidence_summary: The Capability boundary and "User-Facing Output" say turn-based and bounded goal-style loops *can run in the current session*, yet all three examples are non-runnable from this skill's perspective: Example 1 is time-based (gated on a runner), Example 2 is a pushback on a vague goal, Example 3 is time-based. The "Good goal examples" block gives goal *statements* but no worked design→execute trace.
raw.impact: A reader/agent has no model of the full output it is actually permitted to produce end-to-end (Loop Contract + Execution Plan + Checkpoint + Stop Rule) for the loop types it runs. This is a completeness gap for the intended reader.
raw.suggested_action: Add one bounded goal-loop example that can execute in-session (e.g., "make the failing suite pass, ≤4 attempts, report smallest remaining failure") showing contract, plan, a checkpoint, and the stop-with-evidence rule.
raw.raw_text:
- File: `SKILL.md` (section "Examples")
- Evidence: The Capability boundary and "User-Facing Output" say turn-based and bounded goal-style loops *can run in the current session*, yet all three examples are non-runnable from this skill's perspective: Example 1 is time-based (gated on a runner), Example 2 is a pushback on a vague goal, Example 3 is time-based. The "Good goal examples" block gives goal *statements* but no worked design→execute trace.
- Impact: A reader/agent has no model of the full output it is actually permitted to produce end-to-end (Loop Contract + Execution Plan + Checkpoint + Stop Rule) for the loop types it runs. This is a completeness gap for the intended reader.
- Suggested fix: Add one bounded goal-loop example that can execute in-session (e.g., "make the failing suite pass, ≤4 attempts, report smallest remaining failure") showing contract, plan, a checkpoint, and the stop-with-evidence rule.

raw.title: Four contract fields are illustrated but not defined inline
raw.evidence_summary: `Progress signal means what must change between attempts...` is the only field given an inline definition. `Verification signal`, `Source of truth`, `Evaluator`, and `Pass/fail authority` are shown only by example and overlap conceptually (e.g., in Example 1, "GitHub review thread count plus CI status" vs "GitHub PR checks and review threads" vs "Deterministic GitHub API queries").
raw.impact: An agent filling the contract can conflate *what is observed* (signal), *where state lives* (source of truth), *how it is checked* (evaluator), and *who decides* (authority), producing redundant or contradictory entries.
raw.suggested_action: One-line gloss for each, or a single sentence: source of truth = where; verification signal = what you observe; evaluator = how you check; pass/fail authority = who decides.
raw.raw_text:
- File: `SKILL.md` (section "Loop Contract")
- Evidence: `Progress signal means what must change between attempts...` is the only field given an inline definition. `Verification signal`, `Source of truth`, `Evaluator`, and `Pass/fail authority` are shown only by example and overlap conceptually (e.g., in Example 1, "GitHub review thread count plus CI status" vs "GitHub PR checks and review threads" vs "Deterministic GitHub API queries").
- Impact: An agent filling the contract can conflate *what is observed* (signal), *where state lives* (source of truth), *how it is checked* (evaluator), and *who decides* (authority), producing redundant or contradictory entries.
- Suggested fix: One-line gloss for each, or a single sentence: source of truth = where; verification signal = what you observe; evaluator = how you check; pass/fail authority = who decides.

raw.title: Capability Gate lists what to verify but not how
raw.evidence_summary: `Before executing a time-based or proactive loop, verify: Runner: ... Persistence: ... Logs: ... Cancel path: ... Credentials: ... Write permissions: ... Recovery: ...` — each item names the thing to confirm, none names the verification action.
raw.impact: An agent can satisfy "Runner" by naming a cron entry without proving it actually wakes the loop, or "Cancel path" by asserting one exists. The gate's purpose (only run after verification) is undermined if "verify" is read as "list."
raw.suggested_action: Add a concrete check for the load-bearing items, e.g., "verify Runner and Cancel path by triggering one controlled test run and confirming it both fires and can be halted."
raw.raw_text:
- File: `SKILL.md` (section "Operational Capability Gate")
- Evidence: `Before executing a time-based or proactive loop, verify: Runner: ... Persistence: ... Logs: ... Cancel path: ... Credentials: ... Write permissions: ... Recovery: ...` — each item names the thing to confirm, none names the verification action.
- Impact: An agent can satisfy "Runner" by naming a cron entry without proving it actually wakes the loop, or "Cancel path" by asserting one exists. The gate's purpose (only run after verification) is undermined if "verify" is read as "list."
- Suggested fix: Add a concrete check for the load-bearing items, e.g., "verify Runner and Cancel path by triggering one controlled test run and confirming it both fires and can be halted."

raw.title: Intra-doc trigger overlap between Time-Based and Proactive
raw.evidence_summary: Time-Based `Trigger: interval or schedule.` Proactive `Trigger: event, queue, webhook, routine, or scheduled scan.` "scheduled scan" / "routine" appear in Proactive while "schedule" is the defining Time-Based trigger; "periodic inbox triage" (time-based Best-for) and "issue triage" (proactive Best-for) are near-indistinguishable.
raw.impact: A chooser with a recurring scan task cannot cleanly decide the type from triggers alone. (The prior *cross-skill* overlap with `diagnosing-bugs` is now well handled by the explicit routing sentence; this is the residual *intra-doc* overlap, hence P2.)
raw.suggested_action: One contrasting sentence — time-based = fixed discrete runs that each complete; proactive = continuous/event-stream processing where each subtask exits but the routine runs until disabled.
raw.raw_text:
- File: `SKILL.md` (sections "3. Time-Based Loop" and "4. Proactive Loop")
- Evidence: Time-Based `Trigger: interval or schedule.` Proactive `Trigger: event, queue, webhook, routine, or scheduled scan.` "scheduled scan" / "routine" appear in Proactive while "schedule" is the defining Time-Based trigger; "periodic inbox triage" (time-based Best-for) and "issue triage" (proactive Best-for) are near-indistinguishable.
- Impact: A chooser with a recurring scan task cannot cleanly decide the type from triggers alone. (The prior *cross-skill* overlap with `diagnosing-bugs` is now well handled by the explicit routing sentence; this is the residual *intra-doc* overlap, hence P2.)
- Suggested fix: One contrasting sentence — time-based = fixed discrete runs that each complete; proactive = continuous/event-stream processing where each subtask exits but the routine runs until disabled.

raw.raw_text:
The peer reported **no P0/P1/P2 findings**, which I reject as complete only in the weak sense that the doc is acceptable to pass — but the prior-reviewed P2 gaps (undefined "blocked policy," missing in-session example, undefined contract-field glosses, "verify" with no verification action, intra-doc trigger overlap, single-link source attribution) are real and actionable and the peer omitted them. The peer's "No P0/P1/P2 findings" over-asserts cleanliness; the doc is PASS-with-P2-residuals, not zero-findings.

The peer's line-number citations (`SKILL.md:20`, `:143`, `:270`, etc.) could not be verified — I could not read the file to confirm those exact lines map to the quoted text, so I do not endorse the specific line numbers. The substantive claims behind them are correct per the supplied text.

(Could not verify the peer's claim about exact `diagnosing-bugs` description semantics — sibling read was denied — but the routing sentence in the target stands on its own regardless.)

merged.dedupe_key: file:SKILL.md` (section "Operational Capability Gate"):capability gate lists what verify but not how
merged.title: Capability Gate lists what to verify but not how
merged.evidence_summary: `Before executing a time-based or proactive loop, verify: Runner: ... Persistence: ... Logs: ... Cancel path: ... Credentials: ... Write permissions: ... Recovery: ...` — each item names the thing to confirm, none names the verification action.
merged.impact: An agent can satisfy "Runner" by naming a cron entry without proving it actually wakes the loop, or "Cancel path" by asserting one exists. The gate's purpose (only run after verification) is undermined if "verify" is read as "list."
merged.suggested_action: Add a concrete check for the load-bearing items, e.g., "verify Runner and Cancel path by triggering one controlled test run and confirming it both fires and can be halted."

merged.dedupe_key: file:SKILL.md` (section "Loop Contract"):four contract fields illustrated but not defined inline
merged.title: Four contract fields are illustrated but not defined inline
merged.evidence_summary: `Progress signal means what must change between attempts...` is the only field given an inline definition. `Verification signal`, `Source of truth`, `Evaluator`, and `Pass/fail authority` are shown only by example and overlap conceptually (e.g., in Example 1, "GitHub review thread count plus CI status" vs "GitHub PR checks and review threads" vs "Deterministic GitHub API queries").
merged.impact: An agent filling the contract can conflate *what is observed* (signal), *where state lives* (source of truth), *how it is checked* (evaluator), and *who decides* (authority), producing redundant or contradictory entries.
merged.suggested_action: One-line gloss for each, or a single sentence: source of truth = where; verification signal = what you observe; evaluator = how you check; pass/fail authority = who decides.

merged.dedupe_key: file:SKILL.md` (sections "3. Time-Based Loop":intra-doc trigger overlap between time-based proactive
merged.title: Intra-doc trigger overlap between Time-Based and Proactive
merged.evidence_summary: Time-Based `Trigger: interval or schedule.` Proactive `Trigger: event, queue, webhook, routine, or scheduled scan.` "scheduled scan" / "routine" appear in Proactive while "schedule" is the defining Time-Based trigger; "periodic inbox triage" (time-based Best-for) and "issue triage" (proactive Best-for) are near-indistinguishable.
merged.impact: A chooser with a recurring scan task cannot cleanly decide the type from triggers alone. (The prior *cross-skill* overlap with `diagnosing-bugs` is now well handled by the explicit routing sentence; this is the residual *intra-doc* overlap, hence P2.)
merged.suggested_action: One contrasting sentence — time-based = fixed discrete runs that each complete; proactive = continuous/event-stream processing where each subtask exits but the routine runs until disabled.

merged.dedupe_key: file:SKILL.md` (section "Examples"):no runnable in-session example despite in-session execution being primary capabi
merged.title: No runnable in-session example despite in-session execution being the primary capability
merged.evidence_summary: The Capability boundary and "User-Facing Output" say turn-based and bounded goal-style loops *can run in the current session*, yet all three examples are non-runnable from this skill's perspective: Example 1 is time-based (gated on a runner), Example 2 is a pushback on a vague goal, Example 3 is time-based. The "Good goal examples" block gives goal *statements* but no worked design→execute trace.
merged.impact: A reader/agent has no model of the full output it is actually permitted to produce end-to-end (Loop Contract + Execution Plan + Checkpoint + Stop Rule) for the loop types it runs. This is a completeness gap for the intended reader.
merged.suggested_action: Add one bounded goal-loop example that can execute in-session (e.g., "make the failing suite pass, ≤4 attempts, report smallest remaining failure") showing contract, plan, a checkpoint, and the stop-with-evidence rule.

merged.dedupe_key: file:SKILL.md` (intro:source attribution present but fragile unverifiable
merged.title: Source attribution is present but fragile and unverifiable
merged.evidence_summary: The entire conceptual model is sourced to a single walled-garden link: `Source read for this skill: WeChat translation "...", 宝玉AI, 2026-07-07, https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/...`. No canonical English original is cited; the URL is the kind that rots, geo-restricts, or edits silently, and I could not fetch it here.
merged.impact: A future reader/agent cannot verify that the turn/goal/time/proactive taxonomy and the role-separation diagram actually came from "Getting started with loops." If the link decays, the skill's foundational claim becomes uncheckable. (The doc is hedged, so this is verifiability, not an over-claim.)
merged.suggested_action: If a canonical English source exists, cite it alongside the WeChat translation; label the date as "accessed 2026-07-07" to disambiguate from publish date; and inline-summarize the model so the skill does not depend on the link resolving.

The peer's "No P0/P1/P2 findings" over-asserts cleanliness; the doc is PASS-with-P2-residuals, not

merged.dedupe_key: file:SKILL.md` (section "2. Goal-Based Loop":undefined borrowed concept blocked policy embedded stop condition
merged.title: Undefined borrowed concept "blocked policy" embedded in a stop condition
merged.evidence_summary: `Stop: the goal is met, the turn/attempt limit is reached, or the platform-specific blocked policy says to stop.` The opening caveat only neutralizes *command names* from the source article ("Treat platform-specific command names from that article as concepts"), not borrowed *policies*.
merged.impact: An agent filling/executing a goal loop that has never read the source article will either ignore this third stop condition or hallucinate a meaning. Stop conditions are safety-critical; undefined terms there are exactly the "ambiguous steps" the lens flags.
merged.suggested_action: Replace with plain language, e.g. "or a hard platform block (rate limit, safety block, quota) prevents progress," or drop it since the other two conditions already cover the realistic cases.
